Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Statutory College Survey Reports
Cornell University
Allen, Edwin West, 1864-
Ashbaugh, E. T.
Englehart, Fred.
Lord, Edward.
Mann, Albert Russell, 1880-1947.
Perine, E. T.
Updegraff, Harlan, 1874- .
55-2-3314
.3 cubic feet. .3 cubic feet.
1928. Reports. A series of reports on the statutory colleges of Cornell University, completed for the New York State Department of Education in 1928. Collection material in English 4916078
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Laura Finkel
Date completed:
November 2003
EAD encoding:
Laura Finkel, November 18, 2003
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
Cornell University is the land grant institution for New York State. The University was founded in 1865, and much of its original land and endowment came from the Morrill Land Grant Act. In subsequent years, the State Legislature established four statutory colleges at Cornell: Veterinary Medicine in 1894; Agriculture in 1904; Home Economics (now Human Ecology) in 1924; and Industrial and Labor Relations in 1944. While the statutory colleges are affiliated with the State University system and receive state funding , they are not state-run.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
A series of reports on the statutory colleges of Cornell University, completed for the New York State Department of Education in 1928. The statutory colleges during this period included the College of Agriculture, the College of Home Economics, and the New York State College of Veterinary Medicine. The New York State Agricultural Experiment Station in Geneva was also a statutory unit. The individual reports were written by Edwin West Allen, E. T. Ashbaugh, Fred Englehart, Edward Lord, Albert Russell Mann, E. T. Perine, and Harlan Updegraff.
Cite As:
Cornell University Statutory College Survey Reports, #55-02-3314. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
New York State Agricultural Experiment Station New York State College of Agriculture New York State College of Home Economics New York State College of Veterinary Medicine New York (State). Department of Education
